content:
Freelancers, consultants, and gig workers face a unique insurance challenge. Unlike salaried employees, they have no employer-provided group health insurance, no gratuity, no provident fund, and often no predictable income stream. This makes planning for insurance both more important and more difficult.

**The specific risks gig workers face**

Income disruption: A hospitalisation that puts a salaried employee off work for two weeks costs them nothing in salary. A freelancer loses two weeks of billable income. A serious illness can mean months without income.

No safety net: There is no HR department to handle claims, no employer contribution to insurance, and no institutional support.

Irregular income: Premium commitments must fit within unpredictable cash flow.

**Health insurance is non-negotiable**

This is the first priority. A single hospitalisation without insurance can wipe out months of savings. For a freelancer aged 25 to 35 in good health, a Rs 10 to 15 lakh individual health insurance plan costs approximately Rs 8,000 to Rs 15,000 per year — affordable even on a modest freelance income.

Choose a plan without co-payment, with cashless access at major hospitals in your city, and with a reasonable pre-existing condition waiting period.

**Term insurance for income replacement**

If you have dependents — a spouse, children, or parents who rely on your income — term insurance is essential. For a 28-year-old freelancer with a Rs 60,000 per month income, a Rs 1 crore term cover costs approximately Rs 10,000 per year. This ensures your family can sustain their lifestyle for 12 to 15 years even if you are not there.

**Critical illness cover for income protection**

This is especially important for gig workers. A critical illness plan pays a lump sum on diagnosis of cancer, heart attack, or other major illnesses. For a freelancer who cannot work during treatment (which can last 6 to 18 months), this lump sum replaces lost income. A Rs 25 lakh critical illness cover for a 30-year-old costs approximately Rs 5,000 to Rs 8,000 per year.

**Personal accident insurance**

If your work involves travel or physical activity, personal accident insurance provides coverage for death or disability due to an accident. This is very affordable — Rs 10 lakh coverage for approximately Rs 1,500 to Rs 2,000 per year.

**Disability income insurance**

This is the most overlooked cover for freelancers. If you become permanently disabled and cannot work, a disability income plan pays a monthly income for a defined period. Very few insurance products in India cover this well, but LIC's Jeevan Arogya and some private insurers offer partial disability coverage.

**How to structure your insurance as a freelancer**

Priority 1: Health insurance (Rs 10 to 15 lakh) — non-negotiable.
Priority 2: Term insurance if you have dependents.
Priority 3: Critical illness cover (Rs 20 to 25 lakh).
Priority 4: Personal accident cover.

**Premium payment flexibility**

Many insurers now allow monthly premium payments, which aligns better with irregular income. Health insurance can be bought on a monthly basis. Life insurance typically requires annual or semi-annual payments.

I work with several freelancers and consultants in Gorakhpur. If you want a customised insurance plan that fits your income pattern and coverage needs, call 9415313434.
contentHi:
फ्रीलांसर और गिग वर्कर्स के लिए इंश्योरेंस सबसे जरूरी — क्योंकि कोई कंपनी का कवर नहीं।

**प्राथमिकता 1**: हेल्थ इंश्योरेंस (10-15 लाख) — अनिवार्य।
**प्राथमिकता 2**: टर्म इंश्योरेंस — आश्रित हों तो।
**प्राथमिकता 3**: क्रिटिकल इलनेस (20-25 लाख) — बीमारी में आय बदल।
**प्राथमिकता 4**: पर्सनल एक्सीडेंट।

**खर्च**
हेल्थ: 8-15 हजार/साल। टर्म (1 करोड़): 10 हजार/साल। क्रिटिकल: 5-8 हजार/साल।

अपने लिए कस्टम प्लान: 9415313434
